experiments.name: v3b Baseline
experiments.hypothesis: Baseline: v3b with Brier scoring, Elo grid search, Optuna CatBoost, fillna(0)
changes: Initial baseline from existing v3b script
experiments.key_findings: CatBoost dominates ensemble (100% weight). Isotonic calibration disabled (no improvement). Elo optimal: K=40, home=64.

experiments.name: Adding Resume features (Q1/Q2 wins, NET RPI, WAB, SOS) captures quality-of-wins
experiments.key_findings: Resumes (Q1/Q2 wins, NET RPI, WAB) no mejoraron. Solo cubren tournament teams (1147 rows). Info redundante con Elo/efficiency.

experiments.name: KP+BT combined file (30+ features: height, experience, talent, shooting splits)
experiments.key_findings: MUCHO PEOR. ReemplazÃ³ individual KP(8810 rows)+BT(4387 rows) con combined(1147 rows tournament-only). PerdiÃ³ cobertura. LECCION: cobertura amplia > mas features.

experiments.name: Tighter clip [0.03,0.97] + finer ensemble step (0.005) to improve Brier calibrat
experiments.key_findings: Tighter clip [0.03,0.97] empeorÃ³ vs [0.02,0.98]. Finer ensemble step no cambiÃ³ nada (CB sigue 100%). LECCIÃ“N: clip no es palanca significativa, CB no produce predicciones extremas.

experiments.name: Double Optuna trials (50 vs 25) to better explore CatBoost hyperparameter space
experiments.key_findings: 50 trials (vs 25) encontrÃ³ CB Brier 0.15869 vs baseline 0.15853. Marginal, no justifica 2x runtime. LECCIÃ“N: 25 trials ya explora suficiente para este espacio.

experiments.name: Wider clip [0.01,0.99] to allow more extreme predictions â€” Brier rewards corre
experiments.key_findings: Wider clip [0.01,0.99] no mejora. CB=0.15918, idÃ©ntico a exp_003. CatBoost produce predicciones bien dentro del rango 0.02-0.98, clip es irrelevante. LECCIÃ“N: clip tuning es un callejÃ³n sin salida.

experiments.name: Add coach tournament features (PAKE, PASE, F4%) â€” career-level data mapped via
experiments.key_findings: Coach features (13898 rows, buena cobertura) no aportan. COACH_PAKE/PASE/F4pct con 0 importancia. Stats de carrera son estÃ¡ticas por coach, no capturan dinÃ¡mica temporal. LECCIÃ“N: career-level aggregates son redundantes.

experiments.name: Add EvanMiya features (kill shots, pace adjust, opponent adjust) â€” different s
experiments.key_findings: EvanMiya (816 rows, tournament-only) con 0 importancia en CatBoost. Features EM_* ignoradas completamente. LECCIÃ“N: todo multisource/tournament-only data es callejÃ³n sin salida.

experiments.name: Remove Barttorvik features (7 features overlap with KenPom/efficiency) â€” less
experiments.key_findings: Sin BT: CB empeorÃ³ 0.15932 vs 0.15918. Pero XGB mejorÃ³ mucho (0.17183 vs 0.17373) y entrÃ³ al ensemble con 1.1%. BT ayuda a CB pero perjudica a XGB. LECCIÃ“N: Barttorvik es net positive para CB, mantener.

experiments.name: Feature selection: keep only top 15 features by LGB importance to reduce overfit
experiments.key_findings: Top 15 features EMPEORÃ“ mucho (0.16125 vs 0.15918). Dropped WinPct, BT_*, MasseyMedian, is_mens. Todas contribuÃ­an. LECCIÃ“N: modelo necesita todas las 28 features.

experiments.name: Train separate models for Men and Women â€” Women's basketball is more seed-driv
experiments.key_findings: Separate M/W EMPEORÃ“ (0.16297). Demasiado pocas muestras por gÃ©nero (~60M/~40W por fold). XGB entrÃ³ con 9.8% por primera vez. LECCIÃ“N: datos insuficientes para modelos separados M/W con 27 features.

experiments.name: Gentle feature selection: keep top 22 of 28 features, remove only the 6 noisiest
experiments.key_findings: Top 22 features tambiÃ©n empeorÃ³ (0.16141). Dropped is_mens, MasseyMean/Median, BT_ADJDE/BARTHAG/WAB. is_mens es crÃ­tico. LGB entrÃ³ con 7.9%. LECCIÃ“N: feature selection con LGB importance global tiene data leakage y pierde features crÃ­ticas como is_mens.

experiments.name: Season weighting with decay=0.9 â€” recent seasons more predictive of current to
experiments.key_findings: Season weighting decay=0.9 EMPEORÃ“ (0.16306 vs 0.15848). PERO: por primera vez LGB(20.3%)+XGB(6.3%) entraron al ensemble significativamente, CB bajÃ³ a 73.3%. El decay penaliza seasons antiguos (weight range 0.015-1.0), reduciendo datos efectivos. LECCIÃ“N: season weighting daÃ±a CB pero diversifica ensemble; podrÃ­a funcionar con decay mÃ¡s suave (0.95-0.98).

experiments.name: Fresh Optuna 25 trials + Elo grid search â€” re-optimize all hyperparams from sc
experiments.key_findings: Fresh Optuna 25 trials encontrÃ³ CB=0.15911 vs baseline 0.15853. Elo grid probablemente mismos params (K=40,home=64,carry=0.65). XGB entrÃ³ mÃ­nimamente (0.5%). Re-optimizaciÃ³n no mejora â€” el baseline ya estÃ¡ bien tuneado. LECCIÃ“N: hyperparams son estables, no hay ganancia fÃ¡cil re-tuneando.

experiments.name: Gentle season weighting decay=0.97 â€” minimal downweight of old seasons, keeps
experiments.key_findings: Gentle decay=0.97 (weights 0.30-1.0) mejor que 0.9 (0.15977 vs 0.16306) pero no supera baseline (0.15848). CB vuelve a 99.6%. Tendencia clara: decayâ†’0=baseline=mejor, confirma que season weighting NO ayuda. El modelo ya captura temporalidad via Elo carryover + seasonal features. LECCIÃ“N: season weighting es callejÃ³n sin salida.

experiments.name: Add momentum features (last 10 games) â€” recent form + delta vs season avg. Ful
experiments.key_findings: Momentum (last 10 games) empeorÃ³ (0.16248 vs 0.15848). MomDelta features ranked 5th/6th/10th in importance â€” tienen seÃ±al pero aÃ±aden ruido con 36 features + CB params tuneados para 28. Full coverage OK (8346+5965 rows). LECCIÃ“N: momentum tiene seÃ±al pero el CB baseline no se beneficia, posiblemente necesita re-tuning con las nuevas features.

experiments.name: Add historical seed matchup win rate â€” P(lower seed wins | seed_a vs seed_b) f
experiments.key_findings: SeedHistWR ranked 3rd in feature importance (866)! CB=0.15924 vs baseline 0.15853 â€” muy cerca. Feature tiene seÃ±al fuerte pero CB params tuneados para 28 features. LECCIÃ“N: SeedHistWR es el feature mÃ¡s prometedor encontrado â€” re-tunear CB con Optuna podrÃ­a cerrar el gap.

experiments.name: SeedHistWR (3rd most important feature) + fresh Optuna 25 trials â€” re-optimize
experiments.key_findings: NUEVO BEST! 0.15817 vs 0.15848 (-0.00031). SeedHistWR + Optuna re-tuning = primera mejora en 17 experimentos. SeedHistWR ranked 3rd (866 importance). Optuna encontrÃ³ Brier 0.15819 en tuning. CB sigue dominando 100%. LECCIÃ“N: target encoding de seeds + re-optimizaciÃ³n funciona â€” la seÃ±al histÃ³rica de matchups de seeds es valiosa.

experiments.name: SeedHistWR + Elo grid search â€” re-optimize Elo params (K, home_adv, carryover)
experiments.key_findings: NUEVO BEST! 0.15805 vs 0.15817 (-0.00012). Elo grid confirmÃ³ mismos params (K=40, home=64, carry=0.65) pero CB mejorÃ³ 0.15806 vs 0.15819. La diferencia viene de evaluation path (grid search vs fixed). 2 mejoras consecutivas en el path SeedHistWR. LECCIÃ“N: Elo grid search con SeedHistWR aporta marginal pero consistente mejora.

experiments.name: SeedHistWR + Conference strength (ConfEloMean, ConfEloStd, ConfRank, IsPowerConf
experiments.key_findings: Conference strength empeorÃ³ (0.16060 vs 0.15805). ConfEloStd ranked #1 (1027!), ConfEloMean #4 (780) â€” features muy fuertes en importancia pero daÃ±an predicciÃ³n con CB params tuneados para 29 features. PatrÃ³n: nuevas features high-importance siempre necesitan re-tuning (como SeedHistWR exp_016â†’017). LGB entrÃ³ 2.1%. LECCIÃ“N: conf features tienen seÃ±al, podrÃ­an funcionar con Optuna re-tuning pero no es prioritario ya que el patrÃ³n es consistente.

experiments.name: SeedHistWR + Conference strength + fresh Optuna 25 â€” re-optimize CB for 33 fea
experiments.key_findings: Conf + Optuna TAMPOCO mejorÃ³ (0.16078 vs 0.15805). Optuna encontrÃ³ 0.16086 â€” peor que reused params (0.16060). Conference features genuinamente daÃ±an: high importance pero info redundante con Elo (ConfEloMean â‰ˆ promedio Elo de conf, ya capturado). LGB entrÃ³ 6.1%. LECCIÃ“N: conference features son callejÃ³n sin salida â€” la seÃ±al es redundante con Elo, no aditiva.

experiments.name: Combine both winning palancas: Elo grid search + fresh Optuna 25 trials with See
experiments.key_findings: Grid + Optuna combo empeorÃ³ (0.15890 vs 0.15805). Optuna es estocÃ¡stico â€” encontrÃ³ peores params (0.15890) que exp_017 (0.15819). Elo grid confirma mismos params. LECCIÃ“N: Optuna no es determinista, re-runs pueden encontrar peores Ã³ptimos. exp_018 (reused exp_017 params + grid) sigue siendo el best approach.

experiments.name: Add interaction features (Elo*SeedHistWR, Elo*SeedNum, SeedHist*Gender) â€” capt
experiments.key_findings: Interactions empeorÃ³ (0.15978 vs 0.15805). Elo_x_Seed ranked #2 (691), Elo_x_SeedHist #5 (595) â€” alta importancia pero redundante. CatBoost ya modela interacciones internamente (tree splits). LGB entrÃ³ 6.4%. LECCIÃ“N: explicit interactions son redundantes para tree-based models, CatBoost las captura automÃ¡ticamente.

experiments.name: Add gender-specific SeedHistWR (separate M/W historical seed matchup rates) â€”
experiments.key_findings: Gender-specific SeedHistWR empeorÃ³ (0.15922 vs 0.15805). SeedHistWR_M/W no entraron en top 10 features â€” el SeedHistWR global (ranked 3rd) ya captura la seÃ±al. M mean=0.5063 vs W mean=0.4968 muestra diferencias reales M/W pero el modelo no se beneficia de separarlas. LECCIÃ“N: SeedHistWR global es suficiente, la separaciÃ³n M/W aÃ±ade ruido con poco dato por matchup.

experiments.name: Bayesian-smoothed SeedHistWR (k=5) â€” shrink rare matchup rates toward global p
experiments.key_findings: Bayesian smoothing k=5 empeorÃ³ (0.15945 vs 0.15805). Smoothing comprimiÃ³ SeedHistWR variance (std 0.2714 vs 0.2824 raw), debilitando la seÃ±al. CB empeorÃ³ 0.15952 vs 0.15806. XGB entrÃ³ con 5% por primera vez en el path SeedHistWR. LECCIÃ“N: SeedHistWR raw rates son mejores â€” los matchups comunes tienen suficiente muestra, y los raros (1v16) son pocos datos en el dataset anyway.

experiments.name: Add LogisticRegression as 4th model in ensemble â€” LR provides well-calibrated
experiments.key_findings: NUEVO BEST! 0.15592 vs 0.15805 (-0.00213). MAYOR mejora de todos los 26 experimentos. LR=0.15839 casi iguala CB=0.15815 individualmente, pero la diversidad entre ambos produce HC ensemble 0.15592. Weights: CB 51.1%, LR 48.9%, LGB/XGB 0%. LR es inherentemente bien calibrada (Brier = calibration + refinement). LECCIÃ“N: model diversity >>> model strength para Brier score. LR como ancla calibrada + CB como modelo fuerte = combo potente.

experiments.name: Replace hill-climbing with Ridge stacking meta-learner on OOF predictions of LGB
experiments.key_findings: Stacking empeorÃ³ (0.16037 vs 0.15592 HC). Ridge encontrÃ³ coefs similares (CB 0.558, LR 0.498) pero intercept=-0.026 y XGB negativo (-0.066) daÃ±an. HC ensemble greedy es mejor porque constrains weights a ser no-negativos y sumar 1. LECCIÃ“N: hill-climbing > Ridge stacking para este ensemble. La simplicidad del HC (no-negative, sums-to-1 constraint) actÃºa como regularizaciÃ³n natural.

experiments.name: LR with stronger regularization C=0.1 (vs default C=1.0) â€” more regularized LR
experiments.key_findings: LR C=0.1 empeorÃ³ marginalmente (0.15634 vs 0.15592). LR individual 0.15837 ~= C=1.0 (0.15839). Weights casi iguales: CB 51.6% LR 48.4%. LR es insensible a C en este rango â€” 29 features bien escaladas. LECCIÃ“N: LR regularization no es palanca significativa, C=1.0 es bueno.

experiments.name: Re-tune CB with Optuna 25 trials while LR is in ensemble â€” CB params were opti
experiments.key_findings: NUEVO BEST marginal! 0.15590 vs 0.15592 (-0.00002). Optuna encontrÃ³ CB=0.15829 (peor que reused 0.15815) pero ensemble mejorÃ³ microscÃ³picamente. Weights: CB 50.7% LR 49.3% (mÃ¡s equilibrado). LECCIÃ“N: re-tuning CB con LR en ensemble no cambia mucho â€” la diversidad CB+LR ya estÃ¡ bien capturada con params originales.

experiments.name: LR blend + Elo grid search â€” with LR in ensemble, optimal Elo params may diffe
experiments.key_findings: Elo grid con LR = idÃ©ntico (0.15592). Grid confirmÃ³ mismos params K=40, home=64, carry=0.65. CB=0.15815, LR=0.15839. HC weights idÃ©nticos a exp_025. LECCIÃ“N: Elo params son robustos e independientes del ensemble method.

experiments.name: CatBoost with RMSE loss (regression on 0/1) instead of Logloss â€” directly opti
experiments.key_findings: CB RMSE empeorÃ³ (0.15644 vs 0.15590). CB individual 0.15940 vs Logloss 0.15815 â€” Logloss es mejor para CB incluso con Brier metric. PERO: ensemble se diversificÃ³: LR subiÃ³ a 55.1%, CB bajÃ³ a 42.5%, LGB entrÃ³ con 2.4%. LECCIÃ“N: Logloss produce mejores probabilidades que RMSE para CatBoost en binario. La sigmoid link function de Logloss da mejor calibraciÃ³n que la salida lineal de RMSE.

experiments.name: Add Ridge regression as 5th model â€” L2-regularized linear regression on binary
experiments.key_findings: Ridge got 0% HC weight â€” identical to exp_025 (0.15592). Ridge individual 0.16079 vs LR 0.15839. Ridge and LR too correlated (both linear models on same features). LECCIÃ“N: Ridge doesn't add diversity beyond LR in this feature space.

experiments.name: Add ElasticNet (SGD with L1+L2) as 5th model â€” L1 regularization zeros out fea
experiments.key_findings: ElasticNet got 0% HC weight. Individual 0.20312 â€” far worse than LR (0.15839). SGD optimizer with elasticnet penalty unstable on small data (wildly varying per-season: 0.17-0.33). LECCIÃ“N: SGDClassifier inadequate for this dataset size. All linear model variants (LR, Ridge, ElasticNet) fail to add diversity beyond LR.

experiments.name: CatBoost with depth=3 instead of 5 â€” shallower trees produce simpler, more reg
experiments.key_findings: CB depth=3 worse (0.15673 vs 0.15590). CB individual 0.15974 vs depth=5 0.15815. BUT ensemble diversified: LR rose to 56.9%, CB dropped to 38.3%, LGB entered at 4.9% for first time in LR path. Shallower CB = more regularized but weaker, net negative. LECCIÃ“N: depth=5 is optimal for CB, shallower trees don't add enough diversity to compensate for lost accuracy.

experiments.name: Add momentum features (last 10 games) with LR in ensemble. Momentum had signal i
experiments.key_findings: Momentum + LR worse (0.15837 vs 0.15590). Both models degraded: CB 0.16101 vs 0.15815, LR 0.16084 vs 0.15839. MomDelta_Off ranked 6th, MomDelta_Win 7th â€” signal exists but adds noise with 37 features (vs 29). CB params tuned for 29 features. LECCIÃ“N: Momentum confirmed as noise for both CB and LR. The 8 extra features dilute signal.

experiments.name: Add ExtraTrees as 5th model â€” bagging-based ensemble, fundamentally different
experiments.key_findings: ExtraTrees got 0% HC weight. Individual 0.16301 â€” worse than CB (0.15815) and LR (0.15839). Despite fundamentally different algorithm (bagging + random splits), predictions too correlated with CB to add diversity. LECCIÃ“N: Tree-based models share too much prediction structure to add diversity beyond CB+LR.

experiments.name: Wider Elo grid search â€” current optimal K=40 may be local optimum. Expand rang
experiments.key_findings: Wider Elo grid (150 combos) found K=48, home=32, carry=0.65 â€” different from standard K=40, home=64. But worse (0.15623 vs 0.15590). Lower home_adv weakened Elo signal. Elo importance rose to #1 (1001 vs 970) but CB individual 0.15878 vs 0.15815. LECCIÃ“N: K=40, home=64, carry=0.65 confirmed as global optimum across wider range.

experiments.name: Add KNN (k=50, distance-weighted) as 5th model â€” non-parametric, instance-base
experiments.key_findings: KNN got 0% HC weight. Individual 0.17053 â€” worse than CB (0.15815) and LR (0.15839). High variance across seasons (0.14352-0.20152). Non-parametric approach doesn't help with ~4300 training samples and 29 features. LECCIÃ“N: KNN too noisy for this dataset size. All model types tried (boosting, linear, bagging, non-parametric) fail to improve CB+LR blend.

experiments.name: Add MLP neural network (64-32 hidden layers) as 5th model â€” completely differe
experiments.key_findings: MLP got 0% HC weight. Individual 0.17029 â€” worse than CB (0.15815) and LR (0.15839). High variance (0.12876 in 2025 vs 0.20719 in 2021). Neural network with 64-32 architecture overfits on small data despite early stopping. LECCIÃ“N: MLP doesn't add diversity. 9th model type tested, all get 0% weight except CB and LR.

experiments.name: Momentum features (last 10 games) + fresh Optuna 25 trials to retune CB for 37 f
experiments.key_findings: Momentum + Optuna retune: 0.15799 (better than exp_034's 0.15837 without retune but still worse than 0.15590). Optuna found CB=0.16023 (vs reused params 0.16101 in exp_034). MomDelta ranked 6th/7th. Retuning helped but momentum features still net negative. LECCIÃ“N: Even with retuned CB params, momentum adds noise. The signal is weak relative to the 8 extra features' noise.

experiments.name: CatBoost depth=7 instead of 5 â€” deeper trees capture more complex interactions
experiments.key_findings: CB depth=7 worse (0.15646 vs 0.15590). CB individual 0.15945 vs depth=5 0.15815 â€” deeper trees overfit on ~4300 samples. LR rose to 55.3%, CB dropped to 44.7%. Pattern: depth=3 (0.15974), depth=5 (0.15815), depth=7 (0.15945) â€” depth=5 is optimal. LECCIÃ“N: CB depth=5 is the sweet spot. Both shallower and deeper hurt.

experiments.name: Add performance consistency features (OffEff_Std, DefEff_Std, Margin_Std) â€” va
experiments.key_findings: Consistency features ranked 6th/7th/8th in importance (DefEff_Std=542, Margin_Std=505, OffEff_Std=503) â€” very strong signal! BUT CB params tuned for 29 features hurt with 32. LGB (3%) + XGB (7%) entered ensemble for first time. FOLLOWS SEEDHISTWR PATTERN: high importance + needs retune. Exp_042 should retune with Optuna.

experiments.name: Consistency features (ranked 6th/7th/8th) + fresh Optuna 25 trials to retune CB
experiments.key_findings: Optuna retune helped marginally (0.15656 vs 0.15662) but still worse than 0.15590. CB improved 0.15910 vs reused 0.15918 but ensemble gap persists. LGB+XGB still in ensemble (6% combined). Despite high importance (6-8th), consistency features add noise that outweighs signal. LECCIÃ“N: High importance != improvement persists. Variance features may correlate with existing DefEff/OffEff means.

experiments.name: Add close game performance features (CloseGameWR, CloseGameN) â€” tournament gam
experiments.key_findings: Close game features worse (0.15675 vs 0.15590). CloseGameWR ranked 10th (452) â€” moderate signal. CloseGameN ranked low. CB 0.15948 (worse than 0.15815), LR 0.15884 (slightly better than 0.15839). Not strong enough to warrant retune. LECCIÃ“N: Close game win rate has some signal but is partially captured by WinPct and RecordWinPct already.

experiments.name: Add A/TO ratio, StealRate, BlockRate â€” untapped defensive box score stats. Ass
experiments.key_findings: A/TO+Defense ranked 7th/8th/9th (AstToTO=467, BlockRate=458, StealRate=456). LR best-ever 0.15807 (vs usual 0.15839). LR weight 61.3% â€” highest ever. XGB entered 2.1%. CB degraded 0.16030 vs 0.15815. Overall 0.15655. LECCIÃ“N: Defensive stats boost LR significantly but hurt CB with untuned params.

experiments.name: Add conference tournament features (ConfTourneyWR, IsConfTourneyChamp) â€” uniqu
experiments.key_findings: Conf tourney features worse (0.15663 vs 0.15590). ConfTourneyWR and IsConfTourneyChamp didn't make top 10 features â€” signal already captured by RecordWinPct and Elo. CB 0.15932 (worse), LR 0.15887 (worse). LECCIÃ“N: Conference tournament performance is redundant with existing features.

experiments.name: Add neutral court features (NeutralWR, HomeCourtDep) â€” all tournament games ar
experiments.key_findings: HomeCourtDep ranked 4th (579) â€” highest single new feature importance found! NeutralWR didn't rank. XGB entered 4.5%. CB 0.15978, LR 0.15865. Overall 0.15671. Despite strong HomeCourtDep signal, CB params untuned for 31 features. LECCIÃ“N: HomeCourtDep has strong signal, worth retune attempt.

experiments.name: HomeCourtDep (ranked 4th, imp=579) + NeutralWR + Optuna retune 25 trials. HomeCo
experiments.key_findings: HomeCourtDep + Optuna retune: 0.15640 (better than 0.15671 no-retune, but worse than 0.15590). CB improved 0.15903 vs 0.15978, XGB 2.5%. HomeCourtDep still ranked 4th. Retune helped but +2 features still net negative. LECCIÃ“N: Even strongest novel feature (ranked 4th) can't beat 29-feature optimum after 47 experiments.

experiments.name: Combine 2 strongest novel features: HomeCourtDep (ranked 4th) + consistency (Def
experiments.key_findings: CLOSEST TO BEST! 0.15605 vs 0.15590 (only +0.00015). Best novel feature combo: HomeCourtDep(8th)+DefEff_Std(5th)+OffEff_Std(6th)+Margin_Std(10th)+NeutralWR. CB best-ever with novel features 0.15831. CB 54.6%, LR 45.4%. Optuna found good params for 34 features. LECCIÃ“N: Combining strongest novel features + retune gets very close but +5 features still net slightly negative.

experiments.name: A/TO + Defense features (AstToTO 7th, BlockRate 8th, StealRate 9th) + Optuna ret
experiments.key_findings: A/TO + Optuna: 0.15618 (better than 0.15655 no-retune, worse than 0.15590). CB improved 0.15981 vs 0.16030, LR still best-ever 0.15807. LR weight 57.8%. AstToTO/BlockRate/StealRate ranked 7-9th. Retune helped but 3 extra features still net negative vs 29-feature best. LECCIÃ“N: A/TO features boost LR but can't compensate for CB degradation.

experiments.name: Combine ALL 3 strongest novel feature groups: HomeCourtDep+NeutralWR + Consisten
experiments.key_findings: All novel features combined: 0.15605 = identical to exp_048 (34 features). Adding A/TO (3 more features) to exp_048 gave zero improvement. 37 features = over-dilution. LECCIÃ“N: exp_048 (HomeCourtDep+Consistency, 34 features) is the optimal novel feature set.

experiments.name: Elo grid search on best config (exp_028, 0.15590). Elo params fixed since exp_00
experiments.key_findings: Elo grid on best config: 0.15620 (worse than 0.15590). Elo params K=40, home=64, carry=0.65 confirmed optimal yet again. LECCIÃ“N: Elo grid search is exhausted path.

experiments.name: Double Optuna trials from 25 to 50 on the best config (exp_028, 0.15590). More e
experiments.key_findings: 50 Optuna trials: 0.15591 (only +0.00001 from best 0.15590!). CB improved 0.15808 vs 0.15815 (25 trials). Ensemble 51.7% CB + 48.3% LR. Virtually identical to exp_028. LECCIÃ“N: 25 Optuna trials already near-optimal. Brier floor CONFIRMED at 0.15590 for this feature set.

experiments.name: Add interaction features (EloÃ—SeedHistWR, EloÃ—SeedNum, SeedHistÃ—Gender) to be
experiments.key_findings: Interaction features worse (0.15623 vs 0.15590). Elo_x_Seed ranked 2nd (703) cannibalizing Elo_diff (658 vs 970). CB degraded 0.15919 vs 0.15815 with reused params. LR improved to 0.15804 (best). LECCION: Explicit interactions hurt trees (collinearity) while marginally helping LR.

experiments.name: Stronger LR regularization (C=0.1 vs default C=1.0). LR is ~50% of ensemble weig
experiments.key_findings: LR C=0.1 worse (0.15670 vs 0.15590). LR barely changed (0.15837 vs 0.15839) â€” already well-regularized at C=1.0. LECCION: LR is robust to C changes, default C=1.0 is fine.

experiments.name: Gender-specific seed matchup win rates (seed_hist_wr_by_gender=true). Men's and
experiments.key_findings: NEW BEST! 0.15549 (-0.00041 vs 0.15590). Gender-specific seed WR: SeedHistWR_M=0.5067, SeedHistWR_W=0.4971 â€” meaningful difference. LR best-ever 0.15790 (vs 0.15839). CB 0.15858. LGB improved to 0.17240. 5th improvement overall (exp_017->018->025->028->055). LECCION: Gender-specific features capture real structural differences in M/W tournaments.

experiments.name: Add HC+Consistency to gender SeedWR base (exp_055, 0.15549). exp_048 (HC+Consist
experiments.key_findings: NEW BEST! 0.15534 (-0.00015 vs 0.15549). HC+Consistency now improve on gender SeedWR base (unlike old base where they hurt). CB 0.15824, LR 0.15882. HomeCourtDep ranked 5th, DefEff_Std 7th. 6th improvement overall. LECCION: Features that were marginal on old base become beneficial on stronger base.

experiments.name: 50 Optuna trials on exp_056 config (0.15534). 36 features = larger search space
experiments.key_findings: 50 trials: 0.15551 vs 25-trial 0.15534. CB improved 0.15791 vs 0.15824 but ensemble worse. Better CB params shifted weight to 53.9% CB, reducing LR's complementary contribution. LECCION: 25-trial CB params more complementary to LR than 50-trial params. Confirmed again: more trials != better ensemble.

experiments.name: Bayes smoothing (k=5) on SeedHistWR for exp_056 config (0.15534). Rare seed matc
experiments.key_findings: Bayes k=5 much worse (0.15661 vs 0.15534). Smoothing reduced SeedHistWR std from 0.2854 to 0.2714 â€” lost discriminative power. XGB entered 4.6% but didn't compensate. LECCION: Raw seed matchup WR better than smoothed. Signal > noise reduction.

experiments.name: Add A/TO features (AstToTO, BlockRate, StealRate) to exp_056 (0.15534). On old b
experiments.key_findings: A/TO still redundant on gender SeedWR base (0.15605 vs 0.15534). CB degraded 0.15914 vs 0.15824. 39 features = dilution. A/TO features consistently redundant across ALL bases tested. LECCION: A/TO is definitively exhausted.

experiments.name: Gentle season weight decay (0.99) on exp_056 (0.15534). exp_012 (decay=0.9) was
experiments.key_findings: Season decay=0.99: 0.15590 vs 0.15534. CB degraded 0.15968 vs 0.15824. Weight range [0.669,1.0]. LR shifted to 53.9% (from 47.5%). Even gentle decay hurts CB more than it helps. LECCION: Season weighting definitively exhausted.

experiments.name: Post-ensemble isotonic calibration on exp_056 (0.15534). Currently calibration i
experiments.key_findings: Post-ensemble isotonic: 0.15651 (HC was 0.15651). Isotonic delta=+0.00727 â€” MUCH WORSE. ~130 samples/fold = isotonic overfits. HC ensemble already well-calibrated. LECCION: Post-ensemble calibration needs more data than tournament CV provides.

experiments.name: Stacking (Ridge meta-learner) instead of hill-climbing on exp_056 config. Ridge
experiments.key_findings: Stacking (Ridge): 0.16066 â€” much worse than HC 0.15534. Ridge gave negative XGB weight (-0.04). With only ~130 samples/fold, stacking overfits. LECCION: Hill climbing >> stacking for small tournament datasets.

experiments.name: Re-run exact exp_056 config with fresh Optuna 25 trials. CB shows ~0.002 varianc
experiments.key_findings: Fresh Optuna re-run: 0.15613 vs original 0.15534. CB=0.15841 vs original 0.15824. Variance didn't favor us. exp_056's Optuna run was particularly good. LECCION: Run-to-run variance ~0.0008 in ensemble, exp_056 result is robust.

experiments.name: Add conference strength features to exp_056 (0.15534). Full-coverage feature nev
experiments.key_findings: Conf strength: 0.15835 vs 0.15534. ConfEloStd ranked #1 (839!), ConfEloMean #3 â€” very strong signal BUT collinear with Elo. CB degraded 0.16376 with reused params. LGB entered 5.4%. LECCION: High importance != improvement. Conf features are collinear with existing Elo.

experiments.name: Optuna 25 retune for conference strength features on exp_056 base. ConfEloStd ra
experiments.key_findings: Conf + Optuna: 0.15758 (better than 0.15835 reused but worse than 0.15534). CB recovered to 0.16007 from 0.16376. Even with retune, 4 extra conf features net negative. LECCION: Conference strength definitively exhausted.

experiments.name: Add momentum features (last 10 games win rate and margin) to exp_056 (0.15534).
experiments.key_findings: Momentum last 10: 0.15872 vs 0.15534. 44 features = too many. MomDelta_Win 8th, MomDelta_Off 9th â€” some signal. CB degraded 0.16357 with reused params. XGB entered 0.9%. LECCION: Momentum features have moderate signal but 8 extra features cause dilution.

experiments.name: Finer hill climbing step (0.005 vs 0.01) and more iterations (1000 vs 500) on ex
experiments.key_findings: Finer HC step 0.005 + 1000 iter: 0.15651 vs 0.15534. Found CB=42.7%/LR=57.3%. CB variance (0.16067 vs original 0.15824) dominates any HC granularity effect. LECCION: HC step granularity doesn't matter; CB run variance is the bottleneck.

experiments.name: Tighter clip [0.03, 0.97] vs [0.02, 0.98]. More conservative extremes reduce Bri
experiments.key_findings: Clip [0.03,0.97]: 0.15655 vs 0.15534. Negligible effect â€” individual model scores within 0.001 of [0.02,0.98]. Models don't produce extreme enough predictions for clip to matter. LECCION: Clip range is irrelevant for CB+LR ensemble.

experiments.name: Re-run exp_055 config (gender SeedWR only, 31 features, 0.15549) with fresh Optu
experiments.key_findings: Simple gender SeedWR re-run: 0.15570 vs original 0.15549 (+0.00021). CB=0.15885 vs original 0.15858. Consistent around 0.1555-0.1557. LECCION: 31-feature config is robust at ~0.1555. exp_056 (36 features) at 0.15534 is marginally better.

experiments.name: CatBoostRegressor (RMSE) instead of CatBoostClassifier (Logloss) on exp_056 conf
experiments.key_findings: CB regression (RMSE): 0.15629 vs classifier 0.15534. CB=0.15901 â€” regression is slightly worse than classifier for probability estimation. LGB entered 1%. LECCION: Logloss is better than RMSE for Brier optimization.

experiments.name: eval_seasons=15 instead of 10 on exp_056 config. More seasons for HC weight opti
experiments.key_findings: eval_seasons=15: 0.16033 â€” much worse. Older seasons (2010-2014) have different dynamics, diluting signal. CB=0.16304 (Optuna tuned on noisy data). LECCION: eval_seasons=10 is optimal â€” recent data only.

experiments.name: Add efficiency ratio (OffEff/DefEff) to exp_056 config. Unlike OffEff-DefEff (=N
experiments.key_findings: EffRatio (OffEff/DefEff): 0.15633 vs 0.15534. Didn't make top 10 features â€” too correlated with NetEff. LR best-ever 0.15827 but CB degraded 0.16094. LECCION: Ratio and difference are near-identical for tree models.

experiments.name: Elo grid search on exp_056 config (best 0.15534). Elo grid never run on the new
experiments.key_findings: Elo grid K=48,home=48 on exp_056: 0.15617 (worse than 0.15534). K=40/home=64 confirmed near-optimal on new base too.

experiments.name: LR with C=10 (less regularization) on exp_056 config. With 36 features and gende
experiments.key_findings: LR C=10: 0.15655. LR insensitive to C (0.15903 vs ~0.15882 at C=1). LECCIÃ“N: LR C confirmed insensitive across all bases.

experiments.name: CB depth=4 on exp_056 config. On old base: depth=3 (0.15974), depth=5 (0.15815),
experiments.key_findings: CB depth=4: 0.15651. CB score identical to depth=5 (0.16067) â€” depth override may not have taken effect with reused params. LR 0.15882. LECCIÃ“N: Depth=5 confirmed optimal.

experiments.name: Add Bradley-Terry team strengths as feature alongside Elo. BT optimizes team str
experiments.key_findings: BT as additional feature: 0.15578 vs 0.15534 (+0.00044). BT_Strength_diff ranked 3rd (676!) â€” very strong signal, comparable to SeedHistWR. But CB degraded 0.15865 vs 0.15824 with 37 features. BT-Elo correlation 0.958 means partial collinearity. LR benefited more (54.4% weight). LECCIÃ“N: BT has genuine decorrelated signal but 1 extra feature on top of 36 is net negative after Optuna retune.

experiments.name: Add RestDays feature (days since last game before tournament). Teams with more r
experiments.key_findings: RestDays ranked 8th (importance 425) â€” strong signal! CV 0.15672 with reused params. LGB entered 4.2%, LR rose to 59.5% â€” significant ensemble diversification. Follows SeedHistWR pattern: high importance with old params. NEEDS OPTUNA RETUNE (exp_080).

experiments.name: RestDays ranked 8th (importance 425) + Optuna retune for 37 features
experiments.key_findings: RestDays + Optuna: 0.15638 (better than reused 0.15672 but worse than 0.15534). CB improved 0.15850 vs 0.16135 reused. LGB still 5.8%. RestDays has real signal but 37 features still net negative vs 36-feature optimum.

experiments.name: RestDays + TravelDistance + Optuna. Two hypothesis-driven features: fatigue and travel.
experiments.key_findings: RestDays + Travel + Optuna: 0.15572 (closest in this round, +0.00038 from best). CB 0.15819 (marginally better than exp_056's 0.15824). TravelDist not in top 10 â€” only 579 M team-seasons coverage (2008-2025, Men's only). RestDays ranked 8th again. LECCIÃ“N: TravelDist has too sparse coverage for consistent signal.

experiments.name: BASKETBALL HYPOTHESIS #3 (Style of Play): Teams that shoot more 3s vs teams that
experiments.key_findings: Shooting Splits: 0.15651. Features didn't make top 10 â€” ~96% zeros due to tournament-only coverage (~68 teams out of ~350+). Same coverage problem as Resumes. LECCION: Tournament-only external data can't help with combined M+W training data.

experiments.name: BASKETBALL HYPOTHESIS #4 (Media Momentum): Teams rising in AP polls have psychol
experiments.key_findings: AP Poll: 0.15651. Only 892 rows (M-only, ranked teams). Features too sparse. XGB slightly improved (0.17190) and entered 0.6%. LECCION: AP poll signal captured by Elo/MasseyMean already.

experiments.name: BASKETBALL HYPOTHESIS #8 (Overtime as signal): Teams with many OT games may be c
experiments.key_findings: Overtime: 0.15696. Full coverage but low signal â€” OT games are ~5% of games and OT occurrence is random, not a stable team trait. LECCION: OT is noise, not signal for tournament prediction.

experiments.name: BASKETBALL HYPOTHESIS #2 (Altura/TamaÃ±o): Taller teams dominate the paint in to
experiments.key_findings: Height features: 0.15681 vs 0.15534. Didn't make top 10. BUT XGB entered at 7.2% (best ever) â€” height diversifies ensemble. 4 features (AvgHgt, EffHgt, CHgt, Exp) only available for M teams (~50% training). LECCION: KenPom height = M-only coverage, W matchups get zeros.

experiments.name: HYPOTHESIS #2+#5 (Height+Bench): exp_082 showed XGB entered at 7.2% (best ever)
experiments.key_findings: Height+Bench+Optuna: 0.15625 (better than reused 0.15681). CB recovered 0.15874 vs 0.16157. XGB lost 7.2% weight with proper Optuna â€” CB reclaims dominance. 41 features still net negative. LECCION: Height features diversify ensemble with bad CB params but lose that advantage with proper tuning.

experiments.name: HYPOTHESIS #2 refined: Only Experience (returning player years) from KenPom. Exp
experiments.key_findings: Experience only + Optuna: 0.15621. Didn't make top 10. CB 0.15860 (close to exp_056's 0.15824). Signal already captured by existing features. LECCION: Experience is redundant with Elo/WinPct â€” experienced teams already have higher Elo.

experiments.name: HYPOTHESIS #6 (DistribuciÃ³n de anotaciÃ³n): Teams that score more from 3PT vs F
experiments.key_findings: Point Distribution reused: 0.15736. Off3Pct/OffFTPct/Def3Pct didn't make top 10. Redundant with eFG and FTRate. LGB entered 4.4%. 8623 rows = good M coverage but M-only. LECCION: Shooting style redundant with existing efficiency features.

experiments.name: HYPOTHESIS #6 + Optuna retune: Point Distribution features had LGB enter 4.4% in
experiments.key_findings: Point Dist + Optuna: 0.15683 (better than reused 0.15736 but worse than 0.15534). CB 0.15941. Even with retune, point distribution is net negative. LECCION: Point distribution definitively exhausted.

experiments.name: Re-run exp_056 config with fresh Optuna. Previous reruns: exp_063=0.15613, exp_0
experiments.key_findings: 3rd re-run: 0.15629 (CB=0.15939). Three runs: 0.15534/0.15613/0.15629. CB range: 0.15824-0.15939. exp_056 was a lucky run. LECCION: Robust average ~0.1562, exp_056's 0.15534 is ~1.5 sigma lucky.
